hero.eyebrow: Built for Sudanese merchants · Works offline
hero.sub: Sell, track stock, accept Bankak and cash, and print receipts — even when the internet or power drops. AmanaPOS is built for how business really works in Sudan.

problem.sub: Global POS systems assume perfect conditions. AmanaPOS is built for the daily reality of running a shop in Sudan.
problem.p1B: Sales should not stop when the connection drops.
problem.p2B: Your shift keeps running straight through outages.
problem.p3B: No more counting inventory on paper every night.
problem.p4B: Mixed payments, recorded clearly on one sale.
problem.p5B: Every sale is saved and easy to find later.
problem.p6B: Know your day without wrestling a spreadsheet.
problem.solution: AmanaPOS keeps your shop selling, your records safe, and your reports ready — even when the network is not.

feat.sub: Practical tools for kiosks, shops, restaurants, and growing businesses — simple when you start, powerful when you scale.
feat.f1B: Keep selling when the internet or power drops. Every sale is saved on the device and syncs automatically when you reconnect.
feat.f2B: Track stock in real time, get low-stock alerts, and always know what is selling and what is running out.
feat.f3B: Add cashiers, set what each one can do, and follow every shift's sales from one place.
feat.f4B: Accept Bankak, cash, and bank transfers — and split a single sale across more than one method.
feat.f5B: VAT is calculated automatically, with clear daily and monthly reports you can actually read.
feat.f6B: Run many branches from one account, with central stock and transfers between locations.

demo.sub: From tapping a product to a printed receipt, the whole sale takes just seconds.
demo.d1B: Tap items to build the order. Quantities and totals update instantly.
demo.d2B: Choose Bankak, cash, or a transfer — and split it across methods when you need to.
demo.d3B: Print the receipt and save the sale. It syncs the moment you reconnect.

how.sub: AmanaPOS was built to be easy from the first minute. No servers, no long training, no expensive installation.
how.s1B: Use your phone number, verify with an OTP, and add your business name. Simple, fast, and password-free.
how.s2B: Add products, organize them into categories, set prices, and track stock from one clean screen.
how.s3B: Hand the device to your cashier and start selling. Receipts, payments, stock, and reports work from day one.

price.sub: Technology should help your business move forward — not become another heavy cost. No hidden fees, and no percentage on your sales.
price.p1B: For small businesses that want to start organizing their sales today.
price.p2B: For shops and restaurants that need full control over sales, stock, staff, and reports.
price.p3B: For businesses with multiple branches, devices, teams, and custom operational needs.

faq.q1: Can I use it without internet?
faq.a1: Yes. AmanaPOS works fully offline — sales are saved on the device and sync automatically the moment you reconnect.
faq.a2: No. Any Android phone or tablet works. You can add a receipt printer later if you want one.
faq.a3: Yes. Accept Bankak, cash, and bank transfers, and record them all on the same sale.
faq.a4: Yes. Add cashiers, give each one a role, and follow every shift's sales from your dashboard.

trust.sub: The fundamentals that keep your business running, day after day.
trust.t1B: Daily sales are saved locally and synced securely, so your records are never lost — even after an outage.
trust.t2B: Arabic, Sudanese Pound, Bankak, VAT, and offline-first workflows — built in from day one, not bolted on.
trust.t3B: Simple screens designed for fast training and confident daily use, even on a busy counter.

cta.sub: Create your account in minutes. No setup fee, no complex installation.

notFound.title: Page not found
notFound.description: Sorry, the page you're looking for doesn't exist.

about.intro: After the war, many Sudanese merchants lost their shops, tools, records, and years of hard work. But business did not stop — people stood up again, rebuilt from what was left, and kept serving their communities. AmanaPOS was created to support that comeback: a simple, affordable, and reliable point-of-sale system built for Sudanese businesses of every size.
about.mQ: Every Sudanese merchant deserves technology that helps them rebuild, grow, and keep moving forward.
about.mS: We are not building software for perfect conditions. We are building for real Sudanese businesses — unstable internet, power cuts, cash sales, Bankak payments, Sudanese Pounds, Arabic language, and daily challenges that global POS systems were never designed to understand.
about.v1B: Our focus is not only on large companies. AmanaPOS is built for kiosks, grocery stores, spare parts shops, restaurants, retail stores, service businesses, and growing companies that need simple tools to manage sales, stock, and daily operations.
about.v2T: Technology should not be a luxury.
about.v2B: Many businesses are rebuilding from zero. That is why we chose a small monthly subscription instead of expensive setup fees. Our goal is to make professional business tools accessible to the people who need them most.
about.v3T: Local needs are not an afterthought.
about.v3B: Sudanese Pounds, Arabic language, local payment habits, offline challenges, and simple daily workflows were part of the product from day one. We are building software that understands the market it serves.
about.tH: A Sudanese team building for Sudanese businesses.
about.tB: We are engineers, designers, and product builders who believe Sudan's commercial sector deserves better tools. We created AmanaPOS to help merchants organize their work, protect their records, understand their sales, and build stronger businesses in a difficult time.
about.jT: Help us build the future of commerce in Sudan.
about.jB: If you believe technology can help small businesses recover, grow, and compete, we would love to hear from you. We are building more than a POS system — we are helping create stronger digital foundations for Sudan's business sector.

foot.tag: A point-of-sale system built for Sudanese merchants. Sell anywhere — from a phone, tablet, or counter.
